observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management immediately cause the settlement of the long outstanding disallowances of former employees either by collecting directly from them, or by collecting from the persons solidary liable.
observations.managementAction: Disallowances were collected.
observations.reason: Bulk of the disallowances were non-moving for more than 10 years.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to enable the elimination of intra-agency accounts in the succeeding preparation of consolidated financial statements to be compliant with IPSAS 35 – Consolidated Financial Statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up the receivable and the deferred accounts for RPT and SET in CY 2023 based on the list of Real Property Taxpayers prepared and furnished by the Municipal Treasurer indicating the amount due and collectible for CY 2023 and uncollected real property and special education taxes of prior years.
We have recommended and:
The Municipal Treasurer agreed to ensure that the information provided in the list of Real Property Taxpayers, showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible, is correct and approved by the Office of the Provincial Treasurer before furnishing it to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of the Municipality; and
The Municipal Accountant agreed to record the total amounts of collectibles in the setting up of the Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax, and the Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax accounts at the beginning of the year to be provided with allowance for impairment at yearend, using the following pro-forma journal entries:
Loans Receivable - Local Government Units; Due from Non-Government Organizations/People's Organizations; and Other Receivables
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that, until collection is made, all records and supporting documents of all succeeding receivables of the Municipality be in custody to support the receivables recorded and for the fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Office of the Municipal Engineer provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the information on the costs (materials, labor, and fuel) of completed infrastructure assets from CY 2022 and prior years for the adjustment to the correct costs of the infrastructure assets recorded under the Road Networks and Other Infrastructure Assets accounts.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed, with the help of the Municipal Accountant:
For the Loans Receivable - Local Government Units account, to establish the validity of the remaining ₱68,621.81 balance and coordinate with QUEDANCOR or the Department of Agriculture on guidelines for its settlement;
For the dormant Due from Non-Government Organizations/People's Organizations and Other Receivable accounts, to consider implementing COA Circular No. 2023-008, dated August 17, 2023, on the guidelines on the proper disposition of dormant accounts and for the one-time cleansing thereof; and
For the Due from Other Funds account, to immediately return to the General Fund the remaining ₱12,700.00 so as not to constitute misuse of funds.
We have also recommended and the Finance Committee agreed to establish sound and actionable policies for the collection of all past and present receivables.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor endeavor the following:

The Inventory Committee to follow the streamlined procedures in the conduct of physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) for the one-time cleansing of PPE Account Balances, provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006, towards the recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/ missing PPE items to address recurrent discrepancies;

The Office of the Municipal Engineer to provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant with all the information on the costs directly attributable to the construction of the infrastructure assets for the recognition in the accounting records of their correct costs; and

The Office of the Municipal Accountant to comply with the Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) to International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 17 and related COA Circulars specifically on the depreciation and residual value of PPE items.
observations.managementAction:
The one-time cleansing of PPE Account Balances was implemented.

The Office of the Municipal Engineer provided the Office of the Municipal Accountant all the information on the costs of infrastructure projects in CY 2023 for recognition of their correct costs.

The PAG was complied with.
observations.reason: Information on costs of infrastructure assets for periods prior to CY 2023 was not provided.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Through the Municipal Treasurer, prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers indicating the amount due and collectible for the current year and uncollected RPT-Basic/SET of the prior years based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/ Taxpayer’s index card; and

Through the Municipal Accountant, draw a Journal Entry Voucher to set up the correct Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax/Deferred Special Education Fund Tax in CY 2023 based on the amount certified by the Municipal Treasurer for the current year and uncollected RPT of the prior years.  For the succeeding years, only the amount of Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable for the current year shall be set up.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer furnished the Municipal Accountant with the list of Real Property Taxpayers.

The Municipal Accountant set up the Real Property Tax Receivable/ Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax/Deferred Special Education Fund Tax in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Enjoin the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Engineer to work together with the goal of establishing the correct balance of each of the Special Trust Fund to avoid mischarging of the disaster funds;

Ensure that the ₱59,235.00 deficiency be transferred to the CY 2020 Special Trust Fund for appropriation to disaster-related PPAs, and that the cash actually available under the total Special Trust Fund is equal to ₱436,852.49 with adjustments for related expenditures after year-end 2022 up to the present; and

Through the Municipal Accountant, adjust the individual Special Trust Fund subsidiary ledgers to their actual balances, and take up the proposed adjusting journal entries.
observations.managementAction:
It was ensured that the balance of each of the Special Trust Fund was correct.

The deficiency was corrected.

The individual Special Trust Fund subsidiary ledgers were adjusted.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Ensure that the implementation of the remaining 108 development projects totaling ₱13,181,202.56 be fast tracked so that the implementation of future development projects will not be affected; and

Consider the quantity, value, and nature of development projects being funded so that these are proportionate to the capacity of available resources involved, especially the work force.
observations.managementAction:
The implementation of the remaining 108 development projects was completed.

It was ensured that the quantity and nature of the development projects are proportionate to the capacity of the work force.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the help of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, ensure that all planned disaster preparedness and mitigation measures are put in place by implementing all disaster-related programs, projects, and activities within the scheduled timeline before it is too late.
observations.managementAction: It was ensured that measures to reduce and manage disaster risks were put in place.
None.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
correct the accounting books for the unrecognized and erroneously recorded infrastructure assets funded under the LGSF; and
henceforth, upon completion, properly recognize in the General Fund infrastructure assets funded from trust funds.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
prepare the necessary correcting journal entries for proper presentation of the financial statements; and
henceforth, properly recognize fund transfers for specific purposes in the Trust Fund books.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to determine the amount of unexpended current year disaster funds to be transferred to the Special Trust Fund using the RAAO-DRRMF-QRF and the RAAO-DRRMF-MOOE and recognize the same as “Trust Liabilities - Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” and “Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds” to be reflected in the year-end balances of the financial statements.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to diligently monitor the recording of appropriations and expenditures in the Special Trust Fund accounts by establishing a fund tracking scheme where a recorded expenditure corresponds to its appropriation and adjust the existing individual subsidiary ledgers to its actual balances following COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We have recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, agreed to ensure that:
awarded development and mitigation projects be implemented within the timelines; and
efficient canvass and validation of development and mitigation projects be performed.

We have recommended that Management immediately cause the settlement of the long outstanding disallowances of former employees either by collecting directly from them, or by collecting from the persons solidary liable.

observations.managementAction:
Notes to Financial Statements were presented in comparative figures and information.

None.


None.



None.



None.



None.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in cooperation with the Municipal Engineer:

develop strategies to fast track the implementation of all development projects targeted to be implemented within the current year so that no development projects will be forwarded to succeeding years; and

re-appropriate funds from unimplemented development projects, that were considered irrelevant and/or uneconomical, to other priority development projects identified in the Investment Plans to maximize the use of municipal funds.
observations.managementAction:
Implementation as of year-end is 90%.



Funds were re-appropriated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management collect the total disallowances of ₱578,974.43 from the other persons liable if the heirs of deceased persons liable are not amenable to settle as disallowances are a solidary liability of the persons liable.
observations.managementAction: Collected ₱1,600.00 in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

through the Local Development Planning Office, establish the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming, and policy formulation; and

establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation system that will evaluate if applied gender interventions responded to the gender issues identified.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD database was established.



The GAD Focal Person was instructed to review the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation system and recommend to the Municipal Mayor ways and means to better respond to GAD issues.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strictly require the conduct of a complete annual physical inventory of all properties as mandated under the New Government Accounting System. Further, instruct the designated General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain property records and prioritize reconciliation between the recorded amounts in the books with that of the physical count. This measure would reflect the actual condition and correct valuation of assets owned by the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory of properties were conducted at year end.  Property records were maintained.
observations.reason: The physical inventory conducted was incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the accountant of DEP-ED Division Office for instructions on the settlement of the obligation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan coordinated with the DEP-ED Division Office and the latter issued a written reply informing the Municipal Mayor that the matter will be coordinated with the DEP-ED Regional and Central Offices for proper guidance since the account became dormant for almost 20 years and will update the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management instruct the drivers to properly accomplish trip tickets and to submit to the auditor a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each vehicle and Monthly Report of Official Travels.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reports of fuel consumption were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

through the Local Development Planning Office, establish the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming, and policy formulation; and

establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation system that will evaluate if applied gender interventions responded to the gender issues identified.
observations.managementAction: Data gathering is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose the details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statement (FS) in conformity to item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and for a better appreciation of the users of the FS of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to analyze and evaluate the SEDIP and TEEP fund balances totaling ₱845,030.30 to determine the source agency/ies concerned and to remit the same to the SA or the Bureau of Treasury for the appropriate use thereof.
observations.managementAction: The SEDIP and TEEP fund balances were evaluated and remitted to the corresponding source agencies.

observations.recommendation:

the Municipal Accountant to comply with Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and



require the DRRM Officer to include under a separate caption in the subsequent LDRRMFIP and inclusion in the AIP upon approval of the LDRRMC, the list PPAs to be funded by the unexpended DRRM fund balances of prior years in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to maximize the realization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management objectives of the Municipality.

in addition, we have recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit justifications for the 43 implemented DRRM projects not included in the LDRRMFIP, AIP, and supplemental AIP but were implemented and paid from the Trust Liability-DRRM totaling ₱8,805,752.95.

The Municipal Accountant complied with Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

The required Investment Plans were submitted.

(Reiterated in Part II of this Report.)

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce strict implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003 by carrying out the following:

request the legislative body to enact ordinance on the mandatory segregation of wastes, amend the obsolete “no littering” ordinance, and other related ordinances that will support the resilient enforcement of SWM and other environmental laws; and

procure protective gears for the garbage collectors and provide insurance that will safeguard their health.

Mandatory segregation of wastes was effected, i.e. “No segregation, No collection.” Policy.


Protective gears were already provided and SSS memberships were required.

observations.recommendation:

consideration the design and usage of the same;

network by disregarding the 5% residual value;

statements in accordance with PPSAS 17 and COA Circular 2015-008;
and

Due to NGAs and the understatement of Trust Liabilities for the fair

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce strict
implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003 by carrying out the
following:
a. Request the legislative body to enact ordinance on the mandatory
segregation of wastes, amend the obsolete “no littering” ordinance, and
other related ordinances that will support the resilient enforcement of
SWM and other environmental laws;

insurance that will safeguard their health;

efforts to improve the municipal SWM will reach the constituents at the
barangay level; and.
d. Sustain what has been put in place to carry out the law and to think of
ways to further improve their programs, projects and activities for better
protection of the environment and the general welfare of the public.

c. through the Local Development Planning Office, establish the GAD
database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated
data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning,
programming and policy formulation; and

applied gender interventions responded to the gender issues identified.

observations.managementAction: The EO is still updating. Policies on the assessment and approval of loans are strictly followed. There were minor revisions made in the strengthening of the CLEDP.

observations.managementAction: Recording in the books of accounts were made at the time of receipt and utilization. Entries were made. Partially implemented. Communicated with PO; No additional liquidation was made by IFORM. Full liquidation is impossible due to the dissolution of the People’s Organization.
observations.reason: Full liquidation is impossible due to the dissolution of the People’s Organization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management cause the refund of the excess Productivity Enhancement Incentive granted to officials and employees totaling P6,404,850.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the drivers to properly accomplish trip tickets and to submit to the auditor a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each vehicle and Monthly Report of Official Travels;
observations.managementAction: Preparation of monthly reports of official travels have not been implemented.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to consider limiting to only exceptional cases and seasonal basis the hiring of job order personnel, for assignment to other national government offices.
observations.managementAction: Services of Job Orders is necessary in public service delivery however, the LGU finds ways to lessen the hiring based on the needs of the LGU. There are still a number of hired JOs that are not project based as reflected in their accomplishment reports.
observations.reason: There are still a number of hired JOs that are not project based as reflected in their accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to review the LGU’s organizational structure, personnel complement, and convert existing vacant or parallel positions of incumbents in the agency for the creation of IAS, to support the implementation of AO No. 278, as amended by AO No. 70.
observations.managementAction: IAS office was established, however, the proposed Internal Auditor position had not yet been approved. A casual personnel was hired to man the office.
observations.reason: The establishment of an IAS is not in accordance with regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the accountant of the DEP-ED Division Office for instructions on the settlement of the obligation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will not settle the amount considering that the amount pertains to the LGU’s counterpart to the TEEP project it has implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management cause the refund of the excess Productivity Enhancement Incentive granted to the officials and employees totaling P6,404,850.00.

account and book the acquired transferred in the

We recommended Management to:
justify the improper use of cash advances; and for the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-001 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances were only granted for traveling and seminar expenses of Municipal officials and employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management
to:
a. Submit a monthly summary of
leave applications of officials
receiving RATA and the work
schedules or performance

Applications;
b.2 For the members of its
Sangguniang Bayan, minutes of sessions and/or

observations.managementAction: Monthly summaries were submitted as certified by the HRMO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management
to:
a. justify the payments of
monetization of leave credits that are contrary to the provisions of Section 22 and 23 of Civil Service Memorandum Circular No. 41, s.1998 and Joint CSC-DBM Circular No. 2, series of 1997; and
b. formulate an agency guideline or policy for monetization of leave credits that is in accordance with the rules and regulations issued by the Civil Service Commission and Department of Budget and Management, to ensure that officials and employees enjoy the privilege equally and reasonably.
observations.managementAction:
A justification was submitted to the Audit Team.
An internal guideline was formulated but management ensured compliance with CSC rules on the grant of the benefit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to
a. require the HRMO to submit a summary of employees who have availed of the CTO in CY 2016, the date they applied for CTO, the reasons/purpose of the overtime service, the date/s and number of hour/s of overtime rendered; and
b. formulate and adopt an internal policy on the rendition of overtime services and non-monetary remuneration for overtime service rendered that is in accordance with the policies and guidelines under Civil Service Commission (CSC) and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Circulars No. 2, s.2004, No. 2-A, s.2005 and No. 1, s.2015.
observations.managementAction:
Summary of CTO in CY 2016 was submitted to the audit team.
An internal policy was formulated. Special Orders are attached to DTRs of employees availing the Compensatory time-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management exercise prudence and economy in the utilization of government funds by limiting or stopping the practice of undertaking meetings or similar activities outside of their official stations or territorial jurisdiction funded out of public funds and by adhering to the provisions of DILG MC No. 2011-59 and COA Circular No. 2012-003.
observations.managementAction: Only one Out-of-town meeting was done in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend Management to:
a. justify the disbursements; and, b. be vigilant and conscientious in the use of its resources, giving consideration on the austerity measures and saving of government monies for priority endeavors.
observations.managementAction: Justifications were submitted and refunds were made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management avoid the incurrence of the same transaction and require the refund of P257,400.00 from the officials benefited by the expenditure. Also, for the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to revert the accounts affected by the transaction.
observations.managementAction: Payments made were refunded. Payments made in CY 2016 were refunded and accounts affected were reverted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Lacking documents were submitted and complied in the submission of complete contract documents.
observations.managementAction: Lacking documents were submitted and complied in the submission of complete contract documents.

Management:

regulations as well as to the
provisions of COA Circulars
2012-002 and 2014-002, on the accounting and reporting of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund; and

observations.recommendation:

Management:

b. utilize the LDRRMF for disaster risk reduction and management activities pursuant to RA No. 10121, DILG MC No. 2012-73 and NDRRM/DBM/DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction:
Justifications were submitted.
The LDRRMO limited the use of funds to only eligible expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board seek the assistance of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer in the preparation of the Proposed Expenditures for Calendar Year 2017 and every year thereafter, to ensure that succeeding SEF budgets are prepared strictly in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and DBM issuances.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, the Local School Board had prepared the 2017 SEF in accordance with DEP-ED/ DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.2017

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management and the Local School Board limit the utilization of the SEF to the priority programs, projects and activities specified under the provisions of Joint Circulars No. 01 s. 1998, JC No. 1-As.200, JC No. 1-B s.2001 and JC No. 4, s.2014 of DBM/DECS/DILG as updated by DEP-ED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.2017, and Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The utilization of SEF was limited to what has been specified under the provisions of DEP-ED/ DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.2017

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management exert more effort to collect the aforesaid disallowances, including settlement of the NDs under appeal especially from retiring/resigning municipal personnel to be accounted as Other Payables until final decision is passed.
observations.managementAction: Disallowances of employees still in the service are being settled through a monthly deduction in their salaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to strictly require the conduct of a complete annual physical inventory of all properties as mandated under the New Government Accounting System. Further, instruct the designated General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain property records and prioritize reconciliation between the recorded amounts in the books with that of the physical count. This measure would reflect the actual condition and correct valuation of assets owned by the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: PPE ledger cards were maintained by the accounting office. Physical inventory was done per department and consolidated report on PPE was prepared.

observations.recommendation:
a. Excess cash advance should be refunded immediately and receipted at the Treasury Office.
b. Require the refund of the specific cash advance of P9,120.00 and avoid the transfer of cash advance; and
c. Strictly adhere to the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on proper handling of cash advances to ensure efficient and effective management of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Unused cash advances were returned and receipted at the Treasury Office. Cash advance was liquidated. Cash advances were only granted for travelling and seminar expenses of Municipal officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to institute all measures to collect those outstanding receivables and adopt stringent and effective collection policy in the CLEDP program.
observations.managementAction: The CLEDP-in-charge was instructed to serve final collection letters. Efforts were made in collecting despite the difficulty thereof because most organizations were no longer in operation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to issue a pre-numbered Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment for existing property and equipment in the possession of municipal officials and employees and for property to be procured, to require the accomplishment of ARE before its issuance.
observations.managementAction: Pre-numbered ARE were initially implemented for newly procured PPEs. Previously issued PPEs were not accounted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to adhere strictly to the mandated use of government plates and markings and direct the General Services Office to mark all municipality-owned vehicles with the marking “For Official Use Only” (3 inches), under which should be written the name of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Vehicles were already marked with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management the following corrective measures:
a. For every trust fund disbursement, attach the approved POW/Project Proposal/ Work Plan or Physical and Financial Plan to which the expenditure relates; and/or
b. Furnish the office beforehand or during submission of Liquidation Reports the Memorandum of Agreement/s including the accompanying approved project Proposal/s or Work and Financial Plan/s for all projects.
observations.managementAction: The requirements are attached in the liquidation reports. Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Only the Mayor has an assigned vehicle and is not receiving Transportation Allowance.
Management stop granting allowances to officials who use government vehicles and require the refund of the allowances received by the concerned officials as it is not in accordance with regulations.
observations.managementAction: Only the Mayor has an assigned vehicle and is not receiving Transportation Allowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to observe and comply with the guidelines and documentary requirements for government transactions prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001 to ensure that the financial transactions of the LGU were in conformity with laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Accounting office ensured the compliance with the circular.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management:
a. regulate/ control the issuance of travel orders, assess the necessity and reasonableness of the purpose of travel and exercise prudence in granting reimbursements for out of town travels.
b. support all claims with complete documentation and for traveling expense in excess of the P800.00 limit, attach receipts and certifications. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: (a) submit a complete list of all government projects/ programs/ activities that are on-going and those that are to be implemented for CY 2016; (b) inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of an infrastructure project or before the start of the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices have been posted; and (c) regularly submit or furnish the audit team a quarterly status report on government PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly report on PPAs are submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to consider the following measures:
a. Revisit and update the Executive Order creating the CLEDP;
b. Institute concise policies on assessment, approval, terms and conditions; and,
c. Enforcement of existing policies on the grant of loan.
observations.managementAction: The EO is still for updating. Policies on the assessment and approval of loans are strictly followed. There were minor revisions made in the strengthening of the CLEDP. Enforcement of existing policies on the grant of loan were strictly complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management:
a. Thru the Municipal Accountant, to exert effort and work back to trace the reconciling items/ unaccounted difference of P684,785.77 presented in the BRS; and,
b. Make representations to LBP-Lagawe Branch for the correction of the Bank Debit Memo of P150,000.00 noted in the BRS.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were effected. Follow-up was made with the LBP Manager.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management, for the existing Loans Payable of the Municipality, to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for each loan and borrowing and record all transactions particularly releases, repayments and all charges thereon and be guided by the proper accounting of borrowings.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary ledgers per loan accounts with LBP is maintained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management:
a. Recognize the fund transfers as receivable from PO and a payable to the national government by requiring the Municipal Accountant to record it in the books of the Municipality;
b. Communicate with the officials of the People’s Organization to submit Fund Utilization Reports of all completed and accomplished projects and activities as basis for recording the liquidation of fund; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all funds received and utilized. Liquidations.
observations.managementAction: Recording in the books of accounts was made at the time of receipt. Some utilization reports were submitted. Entries were made. Liquidations were partially made but not recorded due to the erroneous accounting of the fund transfer.
observations.reason: Some utilization reports were submitted. Full liquidation is impossible due to the dissolution of the People’s Organization.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management consider setting written rules or guidelines that define the limits of mobile telephone expenses that could be availed by officials and employees considering their positions and actual designations.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines were set in the grant of mobile expense.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
Management:
a. Instruct the designated General Services Officer to assign control numbers to trip tickets and maintain a record of their issuance;
b. Instruct the drivers to properly accomplish trip tickets and to submit to the auditor a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each vehicle and Monthly Report of official travels; and,
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents and to record in the proper asset counts the cost of fuel used in equipment in infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Trip tickets and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were prepared and submitted. Monthly reports of official travels were prepared. Payment for fuel consumption of infrastructure projects were not supported with Program of Works to justify the usage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to consider limiting to only exceptional cases and seasonal basis the hiring of job order personnel, for assignment to other national government offices. The LGU finds ways to lessen the hiring based on the needs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Services of Job Orders is necessary in public service delivery however, most of the hired JOs are project based as reflected in their accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: review the LGU’s organizational structure, personnel complement, and convert existing vacant or parallel positions of incumbents in the agency for the creation of IAS, to support the implementation of AO No. 278, as amended by AO No. 70.
observations.managementAction: The establishment of an IAS was not in accordance with regulations.
observations.reason: The creation of an IAS was disallowed in the review of the LGU budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to account the acquired property and equipment under the trust fund in CY 2015 and prior years and to prepare adjusting entries to book the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant gave assurance to trace back the said PPE and book the same.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the officials paid excess RATA to refund the same and henceforth adhere to existing laws, rules and regulations on the grant of Representation Allowance and Transportation Allowance.
observations.managementAction: DBM Circular was discussed and shall be implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local School Board seek the assistance of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer in the preparation of the Proposed Expenditures for Calendar Year 2017 and every year thereafter, to ensure that succeeding SEF budgets are prepared strictly in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and DBM issuances.
observations.managementAction: Local School Board was provided with a copy of the Joint Circular for guidance.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: For management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. coordinate with the Accountant of DepEd Division Office for instructions on the settlement of the obligation; and b. to take necessary action to properly classify the obligation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will not settle the amount considering that the matter pertains to the LGU’s counterpart to the TEEP project and whether DepEd will pursue the collection or not. Awaiting further action of the DepEd whether it will pursue the collection.
observations.reason: Awaiting further action of the DepEd whether it will pursue the collection; no demand was made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant is amenable to account
and book the acquired property and equipment under the trust fund in
CY 2016 and prior years.

We recommended and Management agreed to justify the improper use of
cash advances; and for the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the
provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-001 on the grant,
utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Submit a monthly summary of leave applications of officials receiving
RATA and the work schedules or performance standards of the
Sanggunian for CY 2016 ; and
b. Support the grant of RATA for the ensuing year with the following
documents:
b.1.For regular officials observing an 8-hour work day, copy of
monthly Daily Time Record (DTR) with supporting Certificates of
Appearance and Leave Applications; and
b.2 For the members of the Sangguniang Bayan, minutes of sessions
and/or monthly work schedule attendance, and leave
applications/certificates of appearance, if applicable.

We recommended that Management:
a. justify the payments of monetization of leave credits that are contrary
to the provisions of Sections 22 and 23 of Civil Service Memorandum
Circular No. 41, s. 1998 and Joint CSC-DBM Circular No. 2, series of
1997; and
b. formulate an agency guideline or policy for monetization of leave
credits that is in accordance with the rules and regulations issued by
the Civil Service Commission and Department of Budget and
Management, to ensure that officials and employees enjoy the
privilege equally and reasonably.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the HRMO to submit a summary of employees who have
availed of the CTO in CY 2016, the date they applied for CTO, the
reasons/ purpose of the overtime service, the date/s and number of
hour/s of overtime rendered; and
b. Formulate and adopt an internal policy on the rendition of overtime
services and non-monetary remuneration for overtime service
rendered that is in accordance with the policies and guidelines under
Civil Service Commission (CSC) and Department of Budget and
Management (DBM) Joint Circulars No. 2, s. 2004, No. 2-A, s. 2005
and No. 1, s. 2015.

We recommended that Management exercise prudence and economy in
the utilization of government funds by limiting or stopping the practice of
undertaking meetings or similar activities outside of their official stations
or territorial jurisdiction funded out of public funds and by adhering to the
provisions of DILG MC No. 2011-59 and COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that Management:
a. justify the disbursements; and
b. be vigilant and conscientious in the use of its resources, giving
consideration on the austerity measures and saving of government
monies for priority endeavors.

We recommended that Management avoid the incurrence of the same
transaction and require the refund of Php257,400.00 from the officials
benefited by the expenditure. Also, for the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Budget Officer to revert the accounts affected by the
transaction.

We recommended that the procuring entity submit and/or attach the
complete copy of the contract together with the bidding documents and
such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of
procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial
review and the technical evaluation thereof on the final payment of the
projects.

We recommended and Management gave assurance that the
Local/Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Plan for the ensuing years had
been formulated, approved and in accordance with the National Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management framework.

We recommended that Management:
a. instruct the LDRRMO to adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 10121
and its implementing rules as well as to the provisions of COA
Circulars 2012-002 and 2014-002, on the accounting and reporting of
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund; and
b. ensure regularity and transparency in government transactions by
avoiding lump sum appropriations and adopt line budgeting that will
specify particularly the projects and activities under the fund.

We recommended that Management:
a. justify the use of DRRM fund on activities and projects amounting to
₱1.8 million; and
b. utilize the LDRRMF for disaster risk reduction and management
activities pursuant to RA No. 10121, DILG MC No. 2012-73 and
NDRRM/DBM/DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Local School Board seek the assistance of the
Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer in the
preparation of the Proposed Expenditures for Calendar Year 2017 and
every year thereafter, to ensure that succeeding SEF budgets are
prepared strictly in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and
DBM issuances.

We recommended that Management and the Local School Board limit the
utilization of the SEF to the priority programs, projects and activities
enumerated under the provisions of Joint Circulars No. 01 s.1998, JC No.
1-As.200, JC No. 1-B s.2001 and JC No. 4, s. 2014 of DBM/DECS/DILG
as updated by DEPED/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, and
Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended Management to strictly require the conduct of a complete annual physical inventory of all properties as mandated under the New Government Accounting System. Further, instruct the designated General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain property records and prioritize reconciliation between the recorded amounts in the books with that of the physical count. This measure would reflect the actual condition and correct valuation of assets owned by the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: PPE ledgers cards are being implemented. cards are being maintained by the accounting office. Physical counting was done per year end. However, reconciliation with the accounting records and physical count is to be taken 1st quarter of 2017.
observations.reason: No submitted Report on Annual Physical Count of PPE as of department. Physical counting was done per year end. However, reconciliation with the accounting records and physical count is to be taken 1st quarter of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to: a) Be stringent in resorting to cash advance as their mode of payment, such that the grant, utilization and liquidations be adequately supported with the necessary documents; b) Excess cash advance should be refunded immediately; excess is required to be receipted at the treasury office. c) Require the refund of the specific cash advance of ₱9,120.00 and avoid the transfer of cash advance; and d) Strictly adhere to the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on proper handling of cash advances to ensure efficient and effective management of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Cash advance is granted only for travelling and /seminar expenses contrary to rules and regulations. There are still Cash Advances that are contrary to rules and regulations.
observations.reason: There are still Cash Advances that are contrary to rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to institute all measures to collect those outstanding receivables and adopt stringent and effective collection policy in the CLEDP program.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the employee in charge of the Loans Receivable – CLEDP to serve final collection letters. There is Loans Receivable–Others/ Due from NGOs/POs - difficulty in the collections for the reason that most if not all organizations were no longer in operation.
observations.reason: There was an 8% decrease/collection for 2016. No collections for 2016 for Due from NGOs/POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to issue a pre-numbered Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment for existing property and equipment in the possession of municipal officials and employees, and for property to be procured, to require the accomplishment of the ARE before its issuance. For a more facilitative identification of property, we have recommended further that management start adopting the installation of Inventory Tag/Sticker or Property Tag on all property and equipment. The Inventory/Property Tags should be attached to the property or equipment and not easily removed.
observations.managementAction: Pre numbered ARE were already assigned and property tags and stickers were procured for 2016. Property tags purchased and stickers were procured but were not yet used. Mostly, no AREs were issued for PPE for 2016. Property tags were procured and ready for installation.
observations.reason: Not all AREs and property tags/stickers were actually issued/used for PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to adhere strictly to the mandated use of government plates and markings and direct the General Services Office to mark all municipality-owned vehicles with the marking “For Official Use Only” (3 inches), under which should be written the name of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Vehicles were already marked with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”.
observations.reason: Not all vehicles were marked.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management the following corrective measures: a. for every trust fund disbursement, attach the approved POW/ Project Proposal/ Work and Financial Plan or Physical and liquidation Financial Plan to which the expenditure relates; and/or b. furnish the office either beforehand or during submission of Liquidation Reports the Memorandum of Agreement/s including the accompanying approved Project Proposal/s or Work and Financial Plan/s for all projects being implemented by the agency under the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The requirements are attached in the liquidation reports. However, there are some trust fund disbursement for which the required supporting documents are absent. Some fund transfers lacked the necessary supporting documents. Partially implemented. Not all liquidation reports were accompanied with the required supporting documents.
observations.reason: Not all trust fund disbursement were supported with the required supporting documents. Not all liquidation reports were accompanied with the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop granting only the Mayor transportation allowances to officials who use government vehicle and require the refund of the allowances received by the concerned officials as it is not in accordance with regulations.
observations.managementAction: Only the Mayor has an assigned MR. No refunds were made. There are still officials who claimed full TA despite having used government vehicle. We noted that gasoline consumption was also incurred by some officials who claimed full TA.
observations.reason: There are still officials who claimed full TA despite using government vehicle; gasoline consumption was also incurred by some officials who claimed full TA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to observe and comply with the guidelines and documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001 to ensure that the financial transactions of the LGU were in conformity with laws, rules and regulations. We further recommended Management to require the submission of the lacking documents indicated in Annex I. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Accounting staff will exercise diligence to comply with COA Cir. No 2012-001. Further, there were terminal leave payments with incomplete supporting documents.
observations.reason: There were terminal leave payments with incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management regulate/control the issuance of travel orders, assess the necessity and reasonableness of the purpose of travel and exercise prudence in granting reimbursements for out of town travels. We recommended management to support all claims with complete documentation and for traveling expense in excess of the ₱800.00 limit, attach receipts and the certifications. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Complied. Partially Implemented. We still noted travels that were not related to the official functions of officials/employees. There were still travels not related to official functions. Some claims lacked certification on hotel lodging in excess of the ₱400.00 limit.
observations.reason: We still noted travels that were not related to official functions and no certification on claims of actual hotel lodging in excess of the ₱400.00 limit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to: a. Require all departments to minimize and/or avoid resorting to payment through reimbursement, except for travels and emergency purchases only; b. Instruct the Municipal Accounting Officer to strictly enforce that all payments for the procurement of goods and services be made by check directly to suppliers, dealers or creditors and to ensure that all claims are duly supported with the necessary documents; and c. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to require at least three canvass/quotations from eligible suppliers for procurements made through shopping or small value procurement.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursement for mobile expenses is sometimes allowed in order that mobile lines will not be cut off. Office is strict in implementation of COA Cir. 2012-001. BIDS and AWARDS committee acted on the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reimbursement for mobile expenses is sometimes allowed; Bids and Awards Committee acted on the recommendation. Office is strict in implementation of COA Cir. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) coordinate with or make representations to the management of depository banks for the early release of monthly bank statements; and (b) direct the accounting department to prepare bank reconciliation statements and to secure and submit the same to the auditor within the prescribed period. We recommended Management to (a) submit a complete list of all government projects/programs/activities that are on-going and those that are to be implemented this calendar year 2016; (b) inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of an PPAs after the award of an infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards will also be posted; and (c) regularly submit or furnish the audit team a quarterly status report on government PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Updated preparation and submission of Bank Statements. The LBP allowed the accounting staff time to get snapshots for all bank accounts while the Bank Statements were not given which are usually two months delayed. The MPDC have required all departments to submit their PPA reports; consolidation of PPA reports pertains to infrastructure projects only.
observations.reason: The MPDC have required all departments to submit their PPA reports. Bank Statements snapshots were allowed by LBP and accounting staff submitted bank reconciliations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to enforce the COA Order of Execution and adopt the settlements through payroll deduction of disallowance in accordance with COA rules, regulations and policies. This is without prejudice to other administrative remedies available to enforce settlement of disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Continuous collection implemented. Minimal settlements were made.
observations.reason: Settlements through payroll deduction are minimal. No significant increase in deductions was made, contrary to management's answer in the previous year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to consider the following measures: a. Revisit and update the Executive Order creating the CLEDP; b. Institute concise policies on assessment, approval, terms and conditions; and c. Be guided with the laws, rules, regulations and other government directives on the transfer of funds to NGOs/POs and Civic organizations.
observations.managementAction: For compliance. No Management action to revisit and update the Executive Order. The LGU stopped granting loans to NGOs/POs and instead utilized the budget for CLEDP for livelihood trainings.
observations.reason: No Management action to update Executive Order and institute concise policies; however, the LGU stopped granting loans and used budget for CLEDP trainings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the accounting and treasury division to properly withhold taxes on benefits of officials and employees to help the government accelerate its collection of income.
observations.managementAction: Computation was made and monthly withholding taxes were made in the payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to submit a copy of the LGU Annual GPB for CY 2016 after five working days from receipt of the approved plan from the DILG-CAR and copy of its Accomplishment Report within five working days to COA.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Programs and Accomplishment Report were prepared and copies were furnished to COA.

observations.recommendation: For the Municipal Accountant to: a. exert effort and work back to trace the reconciling adjustments/ unaccounted difference of ₱684,785.77 presented in the BRS; b. For the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to cancel the stale checks and make the necessary adjustments in their books; c. For Management to make representations to LBP-Lagawe Branch for the correction of the bank Debit Memo of ₱150,000.00 noted in the BRS.
observations.managementAction: Proper reconciling adjustments were made. Adjustments were acted upon. Followed up with the LBP Manager. Some adjustments were made but the reconciling amount remained in the BRS and had changed monthly.
observations.reason: Adjustments were made but the reconciling amount remained in the BRS and had changed monthly. The bank charge of ₱150,000.00 remained as a reconciling item.

observations.recommendation: For Management to: a. Adhere to existing laws, rules and regulation on the provision of government contributions/ share to Pag-IBIG/HDMF benefits of its employees; and b. Adjust the LGU’s contribution to ₱100.00 only per employee per month for 2015 and onwards.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted and reflected in the payroll in 2016 onwards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, for the existing loans payable of the municipality, to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for each loan and borrowing and record all transactions particularly releases, repayments and all charges thereon and be guided by the proper accounting of borrowings.
observations.managementAction: Ledgers for loans are being maintained by the accounting department. Specific ledgers for the different loans of the agency indicating the amount of principal, interest and other charges incurred thereon for specific periods were not made.
observations.reason: Specific ledgers for the different loans of the agency were not made.

observations.recommendation: For management – a. recognize the fund transfers as receivable from the PO and a payable to the national government accounts of the municipality; b. communicate with the officials of the People’s Organization to submit Fund Utilization Reports of all completed and accomplished projects and activities as basis for recording the liquidation of the fund; and c. direct the municipal Accountant to record all funds received and utilized.
observations.managementAction: Recording in the Books of the LGU was made at the time of the fund transfer to IFORM by debiting Cash in Bank and Credit to Due to NGAs. The full liquidation is impossible due to the dissolution of the People’s Organization. Liquidations were partially made but not recorded due to erroneous accounting of the fund transfer. The LGU made entries but full liquidation is not possible.
observations.reason: Full liquidation is impossible due to the dissolution of the People’s Organization. Liquidations were partially made but not recorded due to erroneous accounting of the fund transfer.

observations.recommendation: For Management to cause the refund of the excess Productivity Enhancement Incentive granted to officials and employees totaling P6,404,850.00 and appeal the COA-CAR Decision if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Appealed the COA-CAR decision. No result yet on the appeal on COA-CAR Decision No. 2015-65. Disallowed PEI for 2014 is still under appeal at COA-CAR. Partial settlements were made on the disallowed 2013 PEI amounting to ₱420,215.41.
observations.reason: Appeal pending; partial settlements made on prior disallowances.

observations.recommendation: For management to consider the following: a. Set written rules or guidelines that define the limits of mobile telephone expenses that could be availed by officials and employees considering their positions and actual designations; and b. Charge only telephone expenses used in the discharge of official functions duly supported with complete and authentic documents.
observations.managementAction: Mobile expenses were allowed at ₱2,500.00 for Department Heads, Mayor or Vice Mayor or actual expense as authorized in the Billing Statements. Some employees were reimbursed depending on the discretion of the Department Heads. A written guideline is being formulated.
observations.reason: Some employees also enjoy the benefit reimbursed without limit; written guideline is still being formulated.

observations.recommendation: For management to instruct: a. the designated General Services Officer to assign control numbers to trip tickets and maintain a record of their issuance; b. the drivers to properly accomplish trip tickets and to submit to the auditor a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each vehicle and Monthly Report of Official Travels; and c. the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents of disbursements and to record in the proper asset accounts the cost of fuel used in infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Maintained logbook in the MBO/GSO. Only government vehicles are authorized to use fuels and lubricants. Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption were not properly accomplished and regularly done. Payment for fuel consumption of equipment were not supported with Program of Works to justify usage.
observations.reason: Not all trip tickets have control numbers. Monthly reports were not properly prepared. No Program of Works to justify fuel usage for equipment.

observations.recommendation: For management to: a. intensify efforts in the collection of real property taxes by coordinating with barangay officials for the conduct of information campaign on the timely and advance payment of the tax in order to avail of discounts and avoid penalties; b. take other steps to improve collection.
observations.managementAction: Solicited the help of the Barangay Officials. The Treasury office was able to reach its target.
observations.reason: Solicited the help of Barangay Officials; collection targets were met.

observations.recommendation: For Management to consider to limit only to exceptional cases and seasonal basis the hiring of job order personnel, for assignment to other national government offices.
observations.managementAction: Services of Job Orders is necessary in public service delivery yearly. Most of the hired JOs are project based as reflected in their reports.
observations.reason: Expense for Job Orders is increasing yearly. Most JOs are project-based as reflected in their reports.

observations.recommendation: For management to review the LGU’s organizational structure, personnel complement, and convert existing vacant or parallel positions of incumbents in the agency for the creation of the Internal Audit Service (IAS), to support the implementation of AO No. 278, as amended by AO No. 70.
observations.managementAction: IAS was set up. A casual employee was however hired and designated as Internal Auditor under the office of the Municipal Mayor.
observations.reason: The establishment of an IAS was not in accordance with existing regulations.

observations.recommendation: For Management to require the Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile their records at least quarterly and immediately adjust reconciling items found to reflect the correct and complete monthly transactions of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Constant reconciliation is being done each month before submission of report to COA.
observations.reason: Constant reconciliation is being done each month prior to submission of monthly reports to COA.
